sessions.name: sessions
sessions.description: Core Agent session and history module

session_manager.name: SessionManager
session_manager.description: Decoupled File-System Persistence Repository managing session JSON files and companion sidecars.

agent_session.name: AgentSession
agent_session.description: Pure Domain Entity containing conversation history and session metadata, completely decoupled from persistence or transport.

session_manager_save_session.name: save_session
session_manager_save_session.description: Asynchronously serializes and persists a session and its companion meta sidecar to disk.

side_effects.description: Writes session json and metadata sidecar files to disk

invariants.name: safe_directory_creation
invariants.description: Ensure parent directories exist cleanly by invoking mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) to guarantee idempotency.

invariants.name: deterministic_lifecycle
invariants.description: All read and write streams must be enclosed in with-statement context blocks to guarantee file descriptor cleanup.

logic_steps.name: Compute turn metrics
logic_steps.description: Calculate turn count and ISO-formatted dates dynamically using system times, without hardcoded offsets or magic numbers.

logic_steps.name: Write Sidecar metadata
logic_steps.description: Serialize a separate JSON companion sidecar file tracking counts and metadata securely under context managers.

logic_steps.name: Write JSONL session stream
logic_steps.description: Append or rewrite individual session logs line-by-line using sequentially structured JSONL playback formatting.

modification_tasks.task: Compute session ISO timestamps and sidecar metadata.
Write SessionMetaSidecar JSON payload to disk under with-context blocks.
Surgically serialize chat history list to sequential JSONL stream on disk.

session_manager_load_session.name: load_session
session_manager_load_session.description: Asynchronously loads and parses a session file from disk by ID.

invariants.name: loud_parse_failures
invariants.description: Throw explicit and descriptive ValueError exceptions capturing line number and file path on JSONL line decode or validation failure.

invariants.name: no_magic_literals
invariants.description: Trim, fallback, or read settings must rely strictly on centralized constants (like DEFAULT_SESSION_NAME and MAX_AUTO_NAME_LENGTH).

invariants.name: safe_resource_read
invariants.description: Deterministic file-descriptor handling using context manager wrappers.

logic_steps.name: Read raw streams
logic_steps.description: Read file lines asynchronously using safe thread delegation (asyncio.to_thread) and context-managed stream handles.

logic_steps.name: Line-by-line playback
logic_steps.description: Iterate through individual lines, playing back state modifiers (ChatMessage, SetDelta, RewindDelta) chronologically with early guard clauses.

logic_steps.name: Reconstruct session
logic_steps.description: Load the sidecar metadata to merge dates and reconstruct the fully validated AgentSession model.

modification_tasks.task: Define centralized module constants for name lengths and fallbacks.
Implement sequential play back parser in load_session with early guard clauses and loud parse failure exceptions.
Read and merge companion meta sidecars cleanly during state playback.

bus.name: bus
bus.description: Asynchronous Event Pub/Sub Message Bus core module

invariants.name: ui_decoupled
invariants.description: The bus module must contain zero console print statements except stderr for subscriber exceptions.

logic_steps.name: Establish Module Namespace
logic_steps.description: Establish the engine/bus module with the core asyncio and typing imports.

logic_steps.name: Declare Async Type Definitions
logic_steps.description: Define explicit typing for AsyncListener and establish EventEnvelope/EventType integration.

modification_tasks.task: Establish module namespace `src/engine/bus.py` with required typing declarations (e.g. AsyncListener) and EventEnvelope integration.
Integrate module into the system's package structure and ensure correct imports from engine.types.

message_bus.name: MessageBus
message_bus.description: An asynchronous, thread-safe pub/sub router used to distribute execution telemetry and confirmation requests.

modification_tasks.task: Implement `MessageBus` class `__init__` defining fields for listeners and future tracking.
Implement `derive` method for creating hierarchical subagent message buses with name prefixes.
Implement `request` method using a correlated asyncio Future and uuid4 to coordinate requests and responses within a specified timeout.

message_bus_subscribe.name: subscribe
message_bus_subscribe.description: Attaches a listener coroutine to observe specific event topics.

invariants.name: valid_callback_type
invariants.description: The callback must be a callable asynchronous coroutine listener function.

logic_steps.name: Validate Input Parameters
logic_steps.description: Ensure event_type is a string and callback is a callable AsyncListener coroutine.

logic_steps.name: Initialize Topic Listeners List
logic_steps.description: If the specified event_type is not present in self.listeners, initialize it with an empty list.

logic_steps.name: Append Subscriber
logic_steps.description: Append the callback (listener) to the listeners list associated with event_type.

modification_tasks.task: Implement `subscribe` method that accepts `event_type` and `listener` callback.
Store listener in local `self.listeners` dictionary mapped by `event_type`.

message_bus_publish.name: publish
message_bus_publish.description: Broadcasts an event envelope to all active topic subscribers.

invariants.name: no_silent_subscriber_failures
invariants.description: Any exceptions thrown inside subscriber callbacks must be caught, and their details with tracebacks written to stderr, preventing silent failures and swallowing inside asyncio.gather.

invariants.name: correlation_id_resolution
invariants.description: If a correlationId exists, any matching pending request future in the registry must be completed with the incoming event payload.

logic_steps.name: Normalize Event Envelope
logic_steps.description: Normalize incoming EventEnvelope to a raw dict and set a default sender if not already present.

logic_steps.name: Extract Event Type
logic_steps.description: Extract the event 'type'. If it is missing or empty, exit early without further actions.

logic_steps.name: Resolve Correlated Requests
logic_steps.description: Check if 'correlationId' exists in the message and matches a registered future in self._pending_futures. If found, resolve the future with the message payload.

logic_steps.name: Collect Active Listeners
logic_steps.description: Retrieve the list of registered listeners for the event type and assemble async tasks to run them concurrently, wrapping invocation setup in protective try-except blocks.

logic_steps.name: Concurrently Execute and Log Failures
logic_steps.description: Execute collected listener tasks concurrently using asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True). Check results for returned exceptions and log detailed tracebacks to sys.stderr to avoid silent failures.

modification_tasks.task: Normalize incoming EventEnvelope to raw dict and inject default sender information.
Check for correlationId and resolve matching pending asyncio Future if found.
Concurrently execute listener callbacks with `asyncio.gather(..., return_exceptions=True)` and capture subscriber-level exceptions to stderr cleanly.

uds_server_module.name: uds_server
uds_server_module.description: New asynchronous Unix Domain Socket transport and JSON-RPC 2.0 communication daemon.

active_session_state.name: ActiveSessionState
active_session_state.description: A thread-safe structure mapping an active session context to its running asyncio telemetry and execution tasks.

modification_tasks.task: Define ActiveSessionState dataclass fields with appropriate type annotations.

uds_server_class.name: UdsServer
uds_server_class.description: The Unix Domain Socket daemon listener. Manages the asyncio network socket, incoming reader/writer streams, and active session registries.

modification_tasks.task: TDD & Test Framework: Design and implement a robust integration test suite (e.g., tests/test_uds_server.py) verifying socket startup, client connection lifecycle, request routing, telemetry streaming, and clean teardowns using real asyncio loops and sockets without over-mocking (Guideline 12).

Class Structure & Strong Typing: Create the UdsServer class container with strongly typed properties for socket_path (Path), session_manager (SessionManager), and active_sessions (dict). Initialize active_sessions as an empty dict thread-safely (Guideline 6).

Initialization & Low-level Primitives: Keep low-level primitives parameter-agnostic; configurations must originate at top-most layers. Centralize socket paths and default parameters to prevent premature default assignment and magic literals (Guideline 2 & 3).

Idempotent Socket Binding: In start(), implement idempotent, safe socket file unlinking and binding. Ensure double-initialization is handled cleanly and safely without raising side-effects or leaking socket references (Guideline 13).

Flat Stream & Connection Handling: In handle_connection(), read newline-delimited frames utilizing early exits and guard clauses to keep the 'happy path' flat and readable, preventing deep nesting and arrow code (Guideline 4).

Loud Failure & Error Context: Handle socket reading and decoding errors by failing loudly with rich error context (using JsonRpcCodec to generate JSON-RPC error frames) rather than catching silently or performing silent raw flushes (Guideline 1).

Resource Lifecycle & Async Task Cleanup: In cleanup_session(), guarantee robust cancellation and cleanup of background streaming tasks. Use language-native try-finally or async-with scope managers to prevent task/memory leaks (Guideline 7).

Decoupled Routing & Separation of Concerns: In dispatch_request(), maintain a strict unidirectional boundary. Delegate decoding and encoding to JsonRpcCodec (Guideline 9), and execute core logic separately from transport details (Guideline 5).

Clean Up & Static Analysis: Clean up all unused imports, dead pathways, and update docstrings to represent exact behaviors. Run static analysis (mypy, ruff) and run the full test suite to guarantee high cohesion and focus (Guideline 10 & 11).

uds_server_start.name: start
uds_server_start.description: Starts the asynchronous UDS server daemon. Cleans up any stale pre-existing socket files before binding.

side_effects.description: Unlinks stale socket file and binds the start_unix_server loop

invariants.name: socket_path_configured
invariants.description: self.socket_path must be configured.

invariants.name: server_cleanup_safe
invariants.description: Pre-existing socket file is safely removed before binding.

logic_steps.name: Check running server
logic_steps.description: If server is already running, log informational message and exit early.

logic_steps.name: Cleanup existing socket
logic_steps.description: Check if socket file exists on disk, and unlink/delete it to prevent address-already-in-use errors.

logic_steps.name: Start Unix server
logic_steps.description: Call asyncio.start_unix_server passing handle_connection and path=socket_str, saving the server instance.

modification_tasks.task: Implement start method to check running status, clean up stale socket, start asyncio Unix server with handle_connection, and log state.

uds_server_handle_connection.name: handle_connection
uds_server_handle_connection.description: Manages a client's asynchronous read/write streams. Parses newline-delimited frames and cleans up resources on EOF.

side_effects.description: Monitors socket read streams and handles resource cleanup on client socket disconnect

invariants.name: reader_writer_active
invariants.description: reader and writer must be active stream objects.

invariants.name: resource_cleanup_guarantee
invariants.description: All bound sessions and writer resources must be cleaned up on connection termination.

logic_steps.name: Initialize tracking
logic_steps.description: Create bound_sessions list to track session IDs registered on this connection.

logic_steps.name: Read line loop
logic_steps.description: Loop reading lines from the reader. Break the loop if EOF is read.

logic_steps.name: Parse JSON frame
logic_steps.description: Decode and parse line as JSON. Write JSON-RPC parse error on failure and continue.

logic_steps.name: Dispatch request
logic_steps.description: Dispatch parsed frame via dispatch_request.

logic_steps.name: Cleanup resources
logic_steps.description: In a finally block, clean up all bound sessions, close the writer, and wait for it to close.

modification_tasks.task: Implement handle_connection to manage client stream, parse frames, dispatch requests, and guarantee full resource cleanup in finally block.

uds_server_dispatch_request.name: dispatch_request
uds_server_dispatch_request.description: Core request router. Decodes payload, validates JSON-RPC schema, and invokes session handlers with a flat routing structure.

invariants.name: writer_active
invariants.description: writer must be an active stream writer.

logic_steps.name: Extract request parameters
logic_steps.description: Read frame parameters including msg_id, method, and params, decoding or parsing payload if necessary.

logic_steps.name: Validate JSON-RPC protocol version
logic_steps.description: Verify jsonrpc field is '2.0'. If not, write and drain Invalid Request error (-32600).

logic_steps.name: Validate method presence
logic_steps.description: Verify method is provided. If not, write and drain Method not found error (-32600).

logic_steps.name: Route method
logic_steps.description: Route requests to _handle_session_start, _handle_session_send_prompt, or _handle_session_close, or return Method not found (-32601).

logic_steps.name: Handle errors
logic_steps.description: Catch JsonRpcError or generic Exception, format error payload using JsonRpcCodec, and write/drain onto writer stream.

modification_tasks.task: Implement dispatch_request method to validate JSON-RPC frames, route requests to correct session handlers, and write appropriate error frames on failure.

uds_server_stream_session_telemetry.name: stream_session_telemetry
uds_server_stream_session_telemetry.description: Long-running task that subscribes to the session's MessageBus. Translates and writes telemetry notifications directly onto the client socket stream.

side_effects.description: Streams live formatted telemetry notifications to client socket

invariants.name: writer_active
invariants.description: writer must be an active stream writer.

invariants.name: bus_listener_cleanup
invariants.description: Listener must be unsubscribed from the MessageBus on task cancellation or completion.

logic_steps.name: Initialize telemetry queue
logic_steps.description: Create an asyncio.Queue and define an async listener callback that queues events.

logic_steps.name: Subscribe to event bus
logic_steps.description: Subscribe listener callback to MessageBus event types for telemetry logs and model activities.

logic_steps.name: Process events loop
logic_steps.description: Loop waiting for and retrieving events from the queue.

logic_steps.name: Format and stream telemetry
logic_steps.description: Encode event payloads into JSON-RPC notification frames and write/drain them to client stream.

logic_steps.name: Clean up subscription
logic_steps.description: In a finally block, unsubscribe listener callback from MessageBus to prevent memory leaks.

modification_tasks.task: Implement stream_session_telemetry as a long-running task that queues and streams events, safely cleaning up the MessageBus subscription in a finally block.

json_rpc_codec_class.name: JsonRpcCodec
json_rpc_codec_class.description: Protocol helper to compile JSON-RPC 2.0 frames, responses, error payloads, and streaming notifications, strictly appending newline delimiters.

modification_tasks.task: Design and implement comprehensive unit tests (e.g., tests/test_json_rpc_codec.py) to verify serialization of success responses, error payloads, and notifications, verifying real execution flows without over-mocking (Guideline 12).
Create the JsonRpcCodec class container with class-level constants for protocol version '2.0' and delimiter '\n' to prevent magic inline values (Guideline 2).

Implement method-level argument verification with early-exit guard clauses to raise explicit, loudly failing errors on malformed parameters (Guideline 1, 3 & 4) and use strong python typing (Guideline 6).

Implement clean, pure serialization logic that appends the newline delimiter, ensuring zero side-effects (Guideline 5 & 8) and clean docstrings/comments (Guideline 10). Run static analysis (mypy, ruff) and python unittests to verify.

json_rpc_codec_encode_response.name: encode_response
json_rpc_codec_encode_response.description: Compiles a standard JSON-RPC 2.0 success response payload, appending a newline delimiter.

invariants.name: result_is_dict
invariants.description: result must be a dictionary.

invariants.name: msg_id_type
invariants.description: msg_id must be int or str.

invariants.name: newline_terminated
invariants.description: Returned payload must be newline delimited and encoded.

logic_steps.name: Validate inputs
logic_steps.description: Ensure result is a dictionary and msg_id is an integer or string.

logic_steps.name: Construct response payload
logic_steps.description: Construct standard JSON-RPC 2.0 response dictionary containing jsonrpc, result, and id.

logic_steps.name: Serialize and delimit
logic_steps.description: Serialize the dictionary to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8 bytes.

modification_tasks.task: Implement encode_response to validate types, construct standard response dictionary, serialize to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8.

json_rpc_codec_encode_error.name: encode_error
json_rpc_codec_encode_error.description: Compiles a standard JSON-RPC 2.0 error frame with custom exception names, codes, and diagnostic metadata.

invariants.name: code_is_integer
invariants.description: code must be an integer.

invariants.name: message_is_string
invariants.description: message must be a string.

invariants.name: msg_id_type
invariants.description: msg_id must be int, str, or None.

invariants.name: newline_terminated
invariants.description: Returned payload must be newline delimited and encoded.

logic_steps.name: Validate inputs
logic_steps.description: Ensure code is integer, message is string, and msg_id is an integer, string, or None.

logic_steps.name: Construct error payload
logic_steps.description: Construct standard JSON-RPC 2.0 error response dictionary containing jsonrpc, error (with code and message), and msg_id.

logic_steps.name: Add custom data
logic_steps.description: If optional data is provided, append it to the nested error dictionary.

logic_steps.name: Serialize and delimit
logic_steps.description: Serialize the dictionary to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8 bytes.

modification_tasks.task: Implement encode_error to validate input types, construct standard error dictionary with option data, serialize to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8.

json_rpc_codec_encode_notification.name: encode_notification
json_rpc_codec_encode_notification.description: Compiles a JSON-RPC 2.0 streaming notification frame (no ID parameter), appending a newline delimiter.

invariants.name: method_is_string
invariants.description: method must be a string.

invariants.name: method_not_empty
invariants.description: method cannot be empty or whitespace-only.

invariants.name: params_is_dict
invariants.description: params must be a dictionary.

invariants.name: newline_terminated
invariants.description: Returned payload must be newline delimited and encoded.

logic_steps.name: Validate inputs
logic_steps.description: Ensure method is a non-empty string and params is a dictionary.

logic_steps.name: Construct notification payload
logic_steps.description: Construct standard JSON-RPC 2.0 notification dictionary containing jsonrpc, method, and params.

logic_steps.name: Serialize and delimit
logic_steps.description: Serialize the dictionary to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8 bytes.

modification_tasks.task: Implement encode_notification to validate types and presence, construct standard notification dictionary, serialize to JSON, append a newline delimiter, and encode to UTF-8.

mock_executor_module.name: mock_executor
mock_executor_module.description: Modular mock agent executor containing simulated conversational reasoning, thoughts, and diff generator tools.

invariants.name: separation_of_concerns
invariants.description: The mock executor must not perform any socket communication or serialization.

invariants.name: fail_loudly
invariants.description: Must raise ValueError on error trigger rather than failing silently.

logic_steps.name: Extract MockAgentExecutor
logic_steps.description: Extract the simulation loop into src/engine/mock_executor.py to maintain separation of concerns.

logic_steps.name: Centralize literals
logic_steps.description: Centralize literal triggers, latencies, and payloads into typed variables.

logic_steps.name: Raise errors loudly
logic_steps.description: Ensure any simulated error triggers raise a ValueError with descriptive context.

logic_steps.name: Add unit tests
logic_steps.description: Verify execution scenarios and telemetry via pytest without over-mocking.

modification_tasks.task: Extract MockAgentExecutor from uds_server.py into its own dedicated cohesive module 'src/engine/mock_executor.py' (Principle 5, Principle 11).
Centralize all inline literal mock payloads, keyword trigger strings, and mock latencies into constants or variables (Principle 2).
Add explicit type annotations for parameters and return types across MockAgentExecutor functions (Principle 6).
Ensure requested failures raise ValueError loudly with descriptive context, avoiding silent catch blocks (Principle 1).
Clean up uds_server.py by removing the inline MockAgentExecutor class, updating its imports, docstrings, and references (Principle 10).
Add unit and integration tests specifically testing MockAgentExecutor's behavior across scenarios without over-mocking the MessageBus (Principle 12).

mock_agent_executor_class.name: MockAgentExecutor
mock_agent_executor_class.description: Simulates the asynchronous execution loop of the real LocalAgentExecutor, matching its interfaces and emitting standard bus telemetries.

mock_agent_executor_run.name: run
mock_agent_executor_run.description: Triggers mock agent generation. Parses prompt keywords, sleeps with non-blocking delays to mimic API latency, broadcasts thoughts onto the bus, and saves session files at completion.

side_effects.description: Publishes events like telemetry:thought and telemetry:activity

invariants.name: valid_arguments
invariants.description: session is an AgentSession instance and prompt is a string.

invariants.name: guaranteed_turn_end
invariants.description: MODEL_END event must always be published, ensuring the message bus and session don't get stuck in an active state.

invariants.name: descriptive_error_on_failure
invariants.description: ValueError must be raised with a descriptive context and snippet when error trigger keywords are matched.

logic_steps.name: Validate Input Parameters
logic_steps.description: Assert that 'session' is an instance of AgentSession and 'prompt' is a string. Raise TypeError if validation fails.

logic_steps.name: Publish Model Start
logic_steps.description: Publish a MODEL_START Activity event to the MessageBus containing the prompt length.

logic_steps.name: Process Prompt and Simulate Latency
logic_steps.description: Check lowercase prompt against keywords. If error keyword is present, publish error telemetry log and raise ValueError. If thinking/edit keywords are present, publish respective telemetry logs and sleep for non-blocking latency. Otherwise, publish default response telemetry log and sleep.

logic_steps.name: Publish Model End
logic_steps.description: In a finally block, publish a MODEL_END Activity event to the MessageBus to ensure execution state consistency.

modification_tasks.task: Define mock telemetry payloads, default triggers and latencies
Validate inputs of run function and publish MODEL_START event
Implement prompt keyword parsing and conditional mock telemetry dispatch
Implement finally block to guarantee MODEL_END event is published
Create unit tests in tests/test_mock_executor.py covering all simulation flows

nvim_client_module.name: nvim_client
nvim_client_module.description: Client-side Neovim Lua plugin modules coordinating layouts, keystroke intercepts, and Libuv sockets.

invariants.name: idempotent_setup
invariants.description: Calling setup() multiple times must not register duplicate commands, autocommands, or event listeners.

invariants.name: no_resource_leaks
invariants.description: All active Libuv pipe handles, buffer attachments, and extmarks must be tracked and fully cleaned up on teardown.

invariants.name: fail_loudly_on_errors
invariants.description: Any network failure, deserialization error, or boot failure must raise or log explicit errors with full context, avoiding silent failures.

logic_steps.name: Verify and establish directory hierarchy
logic_steps.description: Ensure the directory tree under lua/code_savant/ exists and matches Neovim's runtimepath search structures.

logic_steps.name: Establish baseline and user configurations
logic_steps.description: Define a default config schema and implement logic to merge user-overridable settings, avoiding inline literals and magic values.

logic_steps.name: Initialize and sequence child sub-modules
logic_steps.description: Construct and orchestrate initialization of nvim_client_init_class, nvim_client_network_class, and nvim_client_ui_class, binding networking streaming events to buffer listeners.

logic_steps.name: Implement setup entrypoint and guard against duplicate execution
logic_steps.description: Create setup(opts) to register custom user commands, autocommands, and buffers, executing early exits and idempotent checks.

modification_tasks.task: TDD Integration Testing Setup: Design and implement integration tests in tests/test_nvim_client_module.lua using busted or a mock Neovim environment to verify integration between boot configurations, socket streams, and UI rendering (Guideline 8, 12)

Directory Setup & File Structure: Create the plugin file layout under lua/code_savant/ (including init.lua, network.lua, ui.lua) with comprehensive EmmyLua type annotations and modular file separation (Guideline 6, 11)

Centralize Configurations & Constants: Move all magic inline literals, static preferences, commands, buffer settings, and namespaces into centralized tables/constants to avoid raw inline values (Guideline 2)

Implement Configuration Bootstrapping: Write the setup configuration merger, keeping low-level socket and UI primitives parameter-agnostic and allowing configurations to only originate from top-level setup() calls (Guideline 3, 4)

Implement Idempotent setup() and Fail Loudly: Implement setup() with clear early exits, guard clauses, and a state check to guarantee idempotent double-initialization safety, raising clean error contexts if required dependencies are missing (Guideline 1, 4, 13)

Resource Lifecycle & Teardown: Design an explicit teardown/shutdown function that safely closes active Libuv pipes, detaches buffer attachments, and removes UI extmark namespaces to ensure no resource leaks (Guideline 7, 13)
Refactoring & Cleanup: Clean up all prototyping leftovers, unused imports/variables, and ensure all docstrings/EmmyLua comments are fully updated and accurate (Guideline 10)

nvim_client_init_class.name: nvim_client_init
nvim_client_init_class.description: Handles the plugin's boot configurations, user preferences, custom commands, and scratch-buffer generation routines.

modification_tasks.task: TDD Setup: Design and implement unit tests first (TDD) using a Neovim Lua testing framework (such as busted) checking boot configurations, idempotent command bindings, and scratch-buffer generation flows (Guideline 8, 12)

Constants & Configurations: Define all default preferences, magic strings, filetypes (code_savant_chat), command names, and buffer options as explicit constants and EmmyLua types to avoid magic inline values (Guideline 2, 6)

Boot Initialization & Input Validation: Implement the main entry-point/constructor that parses and validates configurations, failing loudly with precise diagnostic context if inputs are malformed (Guideline 1, 3)

Idempotent Command Registration: Implement custom command registration (e.g. :CodeSavantChat) and autocommands using early exits and guard clauses to keep happy path flat and safe against double-initialization (Guideline 4, 13)

Scratch-Buffer Generation: Implement the create_chat_buffer handler to allocate a new non-persisted scratch buffer with code_savant_chat filetype, disabled swapfile, and cleanup handlers to prevent memory/file resource leaks (Guideline 7, 13)
Refactoring & Documentation: Clean up any prototype leftovers, remove unused imports or local variables, and provide comprehensive EmmyLua docstrings (Guideline 10)

nvim_client_network_class.name: nvim_client_network
nvim_client_network_class.description: Low-level Libuv binding layer that opens dedicated pipe streams per-buffer and serializes outgoing JSON-RPC requests.

modification_tasks.task: TDD Setup: Implement Busted unit tests for packet accumulator (Guideline 12)
Constants & Types: Centralize magic values as constants and EmmyLua types (Guidelines 2, 6)
Idempotent Connect: Implement connect function safely against double-initialization (Guidelines 3, 13)
JSON-RPC Serialization: Implement send_prompt to serialize and transmit newline-delimited prompt payloads (Guideline 11)
Stream Parsing: Implement on_read utilizing early exits to handle chunk accumulation and decode JSON-RPC frames (Guidelines 1, 4)
Lifecycle & Resource Cleanup: Integrate active pipe tracking to guarantee clean stream teardown and prevent fd leaks (Guideline 7)
Refactoring & Documentation: Clean up dead code and stale comments; add robust EmmyLua docs (Guideline 10)

nvim_client_ui_class.name: nvim_client_ui
nvim_client_ui_class.description: Controls rendering and layout. Formats Nerd Fonts, runs in-buffer extmark toggle offsets, and attaches bytes listeners to restrict input editing.

modification_tasks.task: Design and Implement Unit Tests First (TDD) in tests/test_nvim_client_ui.py checking collapsing, expansion, and bytes interception using high-fidelity Neovim API mocks.
Centralize all configuration constants, namespace names, and Nerd Font glyphs in constants or Typed Enums to avoid magic values.
Implement UI Class Initialization with injected Neovim API client dependency and initialize internal collapsed blocks cache.
Implement on_collapsed_block handler with early return guard clauses and idempotent Neovim extmarks anchoring under a dedicated namespace.
Implement expand_inplace handler to locate extmarks, inject cached block text inline, and safely recalculate line range dimensions with verbose error context.
Implement on_bytes_listener interception hook with idempotent attachment to prevent double-listener registration, safely rolling back unauthorized edits targeting historical lines.
Add explicit teardown logic/destructors to guarantee neovim listeners and extmarks are cleaned up, avoiding resource leaks.

nvim_client_init_create_chat_buffer.name: create_chat_buffer
nvim_client_init_create_chat_buffer.description: Allocates a new, non-persisted scratch buffer with filetype 'code_savant_chat' and disabled swaps.

invariants.name: uv_executable_precondition
invariants.description: If local '.venv' is missing, 'uv' must be executable on PATH, otherwise fail loudly with clear error details.

invariants.name: idempotent_bootstrap_spawn
invariants.description: Concurrent or repeated calls during bootstrapping must not launch duplicate background jobs.

invariants.name: scratch_buffer_configuration
invariants.description: The returned buffer must have 'swapfile' disabled, 'buftype' set to 'nofile', and 'filetype' set to 'code_savant_chat'.

logic_steps.name: Verify Environment and UV Presence
logic_steps.description: Check if the local '.venv' directory exists at the plugin root. If missing, check if 'uv' is on PATH. If 'uv' is missing, fail loudly and raise an error immediately.

logic_steps.name: Launch Non-blocking Bootstrap Job
logic_steps.description: If '.venv' is missing and 'uv' is available, launch 'uv sync' in the plugin root as an asynchronous, non-blocking background job if no bootstrap is already in progress.

logic_steps.name: Allocate Scratch Buffer
logic_steps.description: Invoke vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true) to allocate a new unlisted scratch buffer on Neovim's side.

logic_steps.name: Apply Buffer-Local Options
logic_steps.description: Configure the allocated buffer options: disable swapfile, set buftype to 'nofile', bufhidden to 'hide', and filetype to 'code_savant_chat' using centralized string constants.

logic_steps.name: Bind Cleanup Autocommands
logic_steps.description: Set up buffer-local autocommands (e.g., BufWipeout) to ensure all bound resources and listeners are cleaned up cleanly on buffer close, preventing resource leaks.

modification_tasks.task: TDD Unit Testing Setup: Design and implement robust unit tests in spec/nvim_client_init_spec.lua (using busted) verifying local '.venv' checks, loud errors when 'uv' is missing from PATH, asynchronous non-blocking jobstart execution of 'uv sync', scratch buffer creation, and correct options configuration (Guideline 1, 8, 12).

Centralize Configuration & Constants: Declare all static configuration values including filetype ('code_savant_chat'), buftype ('nofile'), bufhidden ('hide'), and the virtual environment folder name ('.venv') as explicit constants to avoid raw inline values (Guideline 2).

Implement Bootstrapping Environment Checks: In create_chat_buffer (or nvim_client_init_class bootstrapping phase), check if '.venv' exists in the plugin's root. Use early exits and guard clauses to keep the flow flat and readable. If '.venv' is missing, verify 'uv' presence using vim.fn.executable('uv'), failing loudly with an explicit error if it's missing (Guideline 1, 4).

Implement Asynchronous Bootstrap Job: Spawn 'uv sync' as a non-blocking background job using vim.fn.jobstart or vim.system when '.venv' is missing and 'uv' is on PATH. Track the bootstrap job status on nvim_client_init_class (e.g., self.bootstrap_in_progress) to prevent spawning duplicate background jobs concurrently (Guideline 13).

Implement Scratch Buffer Allocation: Allocate an unlisted scratch buffer using vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true). Apply buffer-local options (buftype, swapfile, bufhidden, and filetype) using centralized constants (Guideline 3).

Bind Cleanup Lifecycle Autocommands: Setup buffer-local BufWipeout autocommands to automatically release references and clean up associated listeners or background job resources when the buffer is closed, avoiding resource and file descriptor leaks (Guideline 7).
Refactoring & Documentation: Clean up any obsolete prototyping leftovers, remove unused imports/variables, and supply full EmmyLua type annotations and docstrings (Guideline 10).

nvim_client_network_connect.name: connect
nvim_client_network_connect.description: Asynchronously opens a dedicated Libuv pipe client stream mapped directly to a buffer.

side_effects.description: Establishes client connection to the socket path

invariants.name: non_empty_socket_path
invariants.description: The socket_path argument must be a non-empty string.

invariants.name: valid_bufnr
invariants.description: The bufnr argument must be a valid non-negative integer.

invariants.name: idempotent_connection
invariants.description: No redundant Libuv pipes or connection descriptors are leaked if connect is invoked multiple times for the same buffer.

invariants.name: loud_connection_failure
invariants.description: All socket connection errors in the callback must be checked and thrown/logged loudly with full context.

logic_steps.name: Parameter Validation
logic_steps.description: Validate that socket_path is a non-empty string and bufnr is a valid positive integer >= 0. Fail loudly with rich context if invalid.

logic_steps.name: Idempotency Check
logic_steps.description: Check if a network connection already exists for the given buffer. If a connection is already active, safely handle double-initialization to avoid descriptor/resource leaks.

logic_steps.name: Initialize Libuv Pipe
logic_steps.description: Create a new Libuv pipe object (e.g. vim.loop.new_pipe(false)) to act as our local socket client stream.

logic_steps.name: Establish Connection
logic_steps.description: Invoke the Libuv pipe's connect method asynchronously using the specified socket_path, supplying a connection callback.

logic_steps.name: Register Read Callback
logic_steps.description: Immediately invoke pipe:read_start using the on_read_callback to listen for incoming server-sent frames, ensuring zero packet loss.

logic_steps.name: Return Pipe Handle
logic_steps.description: Return the active, connecting pipe handle to the caller for state tracking.

modification_tasks.task: TDD connect: Implement Busted unit tests in tests/test_nvim_client_network_connect.lua to verify parameter validation, connection setup, and Mock Libuv loop (Guideline 12)
Idempotent connect: Guard against double-initialization of socket connections per-bufnr using a connection registry (Guideline 13)
Libuv connect implementation: Asynchronously create and connect the Libuv pipe handle, handle connection failures loudly with context (Guideline 1)
Stream reading start: Bind on_read handler immediately upon connection to ensure no incoming data frames are missed (Guideline 7)

nvim_client_network_send_prompt.name: send_prompt
nvim_client_network_send_prompt.description: Asynchronously transmits user prompts in a structured JSON-RPC payload across the Libuv stream.

invariants.name: valid_active_pipe
invariants.description: The pipe argument must be a valid, active Libuv pipe handle.

invariants.name: non_empty_prompt
invariants.description: The prompt argument must be a non-empty string.

invariants.name: newline_framing_outbound
invariants.description: All written socket payloads must be terminated with exactly one newline ('\n') character.

invariants.name: loud_write_failure
invariants.description: Any write errors in the Libuv callback must be thrown or logged loudly with full context.

logic_steps.name: Pre-flight Checks
logic_steps.description: Verify pipe is active and not closed, and prompt is a non-empty string. Fail early on invalid parameters with rich context.

logic_steps.name: Construct JSON-RPC Payload
logic_steps.description: Formulate a structured JSON-RPC payload containing the prompt message. Ensure RPC keys and method names are centralized in constants.

logic_steps.name: Serialize to String
logic_steps.description: Convert the structured payload table into a serialized JSON string.

logic_steps.name: Enforce Newline Framing
logic_steps.description: Suffix the serialized JSON string with exactly one trailing newline ('\n') character to satisfy network framing invariants.

logic_steps.name: Asynchronous Stream Write
logic_steps.description: Call the Libuv pipe's write method to transmit the framed payload asynchronously and non-blocking over the socket stream.

logic_steps.name: Write Callback Handling
logic_steps.description: Check for write errors in the asynchronous write callback. Log and throw error with detailed diagnostic context if the write fails.

modification_tasks.task: TDD send_prompt: Create Busted unit tests in tests/test_nvim_client_network_send_prompt.lua simulating non-blocking socket writes and verifying mock payload delivery (Guideline 12)
Payload construction & serialization: Build and serialize the JSON-RPC request payload using typed structures, centralizing method names as constants (Guidelines 2, 6)
Newline framing transmission: Frame the serialized JSON payload with a trailing newline ('\n') and write asynchronously via Libuv pipe write (Guideline 13)
Loud write error callback: Implement write callback that checks for socket errors and propagates them with rich context instead of failing silently (Guideline 1)

nvim_client_ui_on_collapsed_block.name: on_collapsed_block
nvim_client_ui_on_collapsed_block.description: Asynchronously handles collapsed events, caches full content, appends Nerd Font markers locally, and anchors an extmark under a dedicated namespace.

side_effects.description: Mutates buffer to add collapsed virtual lines

invariants.name: id_non_empty
invariants.description: Input 'id' must be a valid, non-empty string.

invariants.name: valid_buffer
invariants.description: The target buffer must be active, valid, and editable.

invariants.name: idempotent_extmark
invariants.description: Only one active extmark can exist for a given block 'id' in the buffer at any time.

invariants.name: non_blocking_draw
invariants.description: UI updates must be scheduled on the main event loop and rendered using non-blocking virtual text.

logic_steps.name: Validate parameters
logic_steps.description: Verify that 'id', 'type', 'title', and 'full_content' are non-empty strings. Return early if invalid.

logic_steps.name: Cache block content
logic_steps.description: Store 'full_content' and block metadata in the local 'collapsed_blocks_cache' dictionary under key 'id'.

logic_steps.name: Verify target chat buffer
logic_steps.description: Retrieve the current active Neovim chat buffer. Throw a loud, descriptive error if it is invalid or closed.

logic_steps.name: Idempotently clear existing extmark
logic_steps.description: Check if an extmark ID for this 'id' is already cached. If so, safely delete it from the buffer using 'nvim_buf_del_extmark'.

logic_steps.name: Format collapsed line marker
logic_steps.description: Build the collapsed line display text using configured Nerd Font glyphs (e.g. ▶, 📁) and styling from centralized constants.

logic_steps.name: Anchor extmark with virtual text
logic_steps.description: Register the new extmark under the dedicated namespace using 'nvim_buf_set_extmark', passing the formatted virtual text options.

logic_steps.name: Cache extmark association
logic_steps.description: Store the returned extmark ID in the cache mapping against the block 'id' for future lookups and expansion.

modification_tasks.task: Design and Implement Unit Tests First (TDD) in tests/test_nvim_client_ui.py verifying cache storage, virtual text formatting, and extmark placement.
Implement input validation and early exit guards to handle empty IDs or null values safely.
Implement logical idempotent cleanup that removes pre-existing extmarks for the same ID prior to placing a new one.
Implement non-blocking virtual text construction and extmark registration using centralized Nerd Font glyphs and highlights under the dedicated namespace.

nvim_client_ui_expand_inplace.name: expand_inplace
nvim_client_ui_expand_inplace.description: Asynchronously expands cached contents inline under the corresponding target extmark, recalculating range dimensions cleanly.

side_effects.description: Mutates buffer to replace virtual lines with cached multiline block content

invariants.name: id_exists_in_cache
invariants.description: The block 'id' must actively exist in the collapsed blocks cache before expansion.

invariants.name: extmark_resolvable
invariants.description: The extmark associated with the collapsed block must be active and resolvable within Neovim.

invariants.name: flat_safe_injection
invariants.description: Inline text expansion must occur within a safe, non-blocking atomic transaction in the main thread.

logic_steps.name: Validate parameters
logic_steps.description: Verify that 'id' is a valid, non-empty string. Return early or raise error if invalid.

logic_steps.name: Lookup cached block
logic_steps.description: Retrieve the cached full block contents and extmark ID from 'self.collapsed_blocks_cache' using 'id'. If missing, throw a detailed, context-rich error.

logic_steps.name: Locate extmark position
logic_steps.description: Call 'nvim_buf_get_extmark_by_id' with the dedicated namespace and cached extmark ID to find the active block's row/line index in the buffer.

logic_steps.name: Format expanded content
logic_steps.description: Split the cached full content into lines and apply visual border decorators or indentation margins using centralized configuration styles.

logic_steps.name: Replace buffer lines
logic_steps.description: Execute a non-blocking replacement of the virtual/collapsed line with the actual multi-line content inside an atomic buffer transaction.

logic_steps.name: Clean up old extmark and cache
logic_steps.description: Delete the old collapsed extmark using 'nvim_buf_del_extmark' and remove the 'id' mapping from the active collapsed blocks cache.

modification_tasks.task: Design and Implement Unit Tests First (TDD) in tests/test_nvim_client_ui.py checking expansion calculations, cached content lookup, and buffer editing.
Implement guard clause checking ID presence in cache, raising clean exception with context if missing.
Implement extmark lookup to locate line numbers dynamically in the active buffer.
Implement atomic replacement of the collapsed line with multi-line content and clean up obsolete extmarks/cache entries.

nvim_client_ui_on_bytes_listener.name: on_bytes_listener
nvim_client_ui_on_bytes_listener.description: Buffer attach hook that intercepts keyboard insertions and deletions, rolling back edits that target ranges prior to the active prompt cursor line.

side_effects.description: Locks historical buffer text regions against modifications

invariants.name: reentrancy_lock
invariants.description: The 'self.is_rolling_back' guard flag must be activated during rollback edits to prevent recursion loops.

invariants.name: read_only_protection
invariants.description: Any change targeting rows strictly less than the dynamic active prompt line must trigger a rollback.

invariants.name: fast_execution
invariants.description: Execution checks must have O(1) time complexity and complete within 1ms to prevent keystroke latency.

logic_steps.name: Check recursion guard
logic_steps.description: If 'self.is_rolling_back' is true, immediately return and do nothing to prevent infinite listener triggers.

logic_steps.name: Extract change range
logic_steps.description: Parse 'on_bytes' callback parameters to get the start row, deleted lines, and added lines of the edit.

logic_steps.name: Resolve protected boundary
logic_steps.description: Retrieve the active cursor or prompt line threshold separating read-only history from current user input.

logic_steps.name: Detect editing violation
logic_steps.description: Check if any part of the edited range lies strictly before the resolved protected boundary.

logic_steps.name: Perform safe rollback
logic_steps.description: If violation is detected, set 'self.is_rolling_back' to true, schedule a silent undo or restoration callback via 'vim.schedule', and reset the guard flag afterward.

logic_steps.name: Notify user of read-only state
logic_steps.description: Display a loud, styled, non-blocking warning to the user's command line indicating that historical context is locked.

modification_tasks.task: Design and Implement Unit Tests First (TDD) in tests/test_nvim_client_ui.py checking rollback behavior, recursion guard, and active prompt lines boundary detection.
Implement the recursion/re-entrancy guard to safely ignore edits triggered by internal rollbacks.
Implement boundary checks using dynamic prompt lines to detect edits targeting read-only history.
Implement rollback execution utilizing 'vim.schedule' to invoke undo safely, and notify user with non-blocking styled text.
Implement clean teardown/un-registration of bytes listener to prevent resource and memory leaks.
Verify that the 'void' primitive is correctly registered as a schema-only DATA_OBJECT in the architecture registry.
Verify that procedures returning 'void' are designed to fail loudly on internal errors (Guideline #1) and fully clean up any context lifecycles (Guideline #7).

any.name: any
any.description: Placeholder for any dynamic or untyped value

modification_tasks.task: Verify that the 'any' primitive is correctly registered as a schema-only DATA_OBJECT in the architecture registry.
Verify that handlers receiving 'any' fail loudly and early with rich context on invalid values, avoiding silent default assumptions (Guideline #1).

dict.name: dict
dict.description: Standard dictionary mapping keys to values

modification_tasks.task: Verify that the 'dict' primitive is correctly registered as a schema-only DATA_OBJECT in the architecture registry.
Validate that consumer components utilizing 'dict' avoid weak/broad typing (Guideline #6) by defining explicit property/item schemas where possible.

AgentSession.name: AgentSession
AgentSession.description: Domain Entity containing conversation history and session metadata

SessionManager.name: SessionManager
SessionManager.description: Decoupled File-System Persistence Repository managing session JSON files

ChatMessage.name: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Structure representing a single chat message in history

modification_tasks.task: Define ChatRole typed enumeration and modern MessagePart schemas.
Update ChatMessage class defaults using native Python 3.14 uuid.uuid7().

SessionMetadataPayload.name: SessionMetadataPayload
SessionMetadataPayload.description: Structure representing metadata associated with a session

uds_server_cleanup_session.name: cleanup_session
uds_server_cleanup_session.description: Cleans up active session resources, cancels background streaming tasks, and removes the session from the active_sessions registry.

side_effects.description: Cancels associated streaming tasks and closes writer

invariants.name: session_id_is_string
invariants.description: session_id must be a string.

invariants.name: tasks_cancelled_safely
invariants.description: Both telemetry and executor tasks must be cancelled and gathered safely.

logic_steps.name: Retrieve and remove session state
logic_steps.description: Pop the target session state from active_sessions dictionary, returning early if missing.

logic_steps.name: Cancel running tasks
logic_steps.description: Invoke cancel() on the telemetry task and the executor task (if present and active).

logic_steps.name: Await cancellation
logic_steps.description: Await tasks using asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True to clean up without raising unhandled CancelledErrors.

modification_tasks.task: Implement _cleanup_session to pop active state, cancel telemetry and executor tasks, and await them safely using gather.
Verify that the 'str' primitive is correctly registered as a schema-only DATA_OBJECT in the architecture registry.
Verify that components consuming 'str' centralize all constants and magic values in variables/enums rather than raw string literals (Guideline #2).

nvim_client_network_on_read.name: on_read
nvim_client_network_on_read.description: Libuv pipe read callback that listens to incoming socket bytes, processes newline-delimited JSON-RPC packets, and dispatches them to UI render handlers.

invariants.name: valid_pipe_handle
invariants.description: The pipe parameter must be a valid Libuv pipe object.

invariants.name: split_packet_resilience
invariants.description: The system must handle partial packets (split packets) and multiple coalesced packets in a single TCP chunk correctly without losing stream synchrony.

invariants.name: newline_framing_inbound
invariants.description: Frames must be extracted strictly based on newline ('\n') boundary demarcation.

invariants.name: safe_protected_decode
invariants.description: JSON decoding must occur within a protected execution boundary, logging failures loudly while keeping the read loop active.

invariants.name: unidirectional_boundary
invariants.description: Processed payloads must be dispatched through callback delegates, keeping the network layer completely decoupled from UI rendering.

logic_steps.name: Loud Error Check
logic_steps.description: Check if err is present. If a socket error occurred, fail loudly, log with rich diagnostic context, and trigger resource teardown.

logic_steps.name: Null/EOF Check
logic_steps.description: Check if data is nil or empty, signaling connection closure or EOF. Exit early and execute a clean stream shutdown to prevent file descriptor leaks.

logic_steps.name: Chunk Accumulation
logic_steps.description: Append raw incoming socket data bytes to a persistent, per-connection string accumulator buffer.

logic_steps.name: Newline Segment Parsing
logic_steps.description: Search the accumulator for a newline ('\n') character. While a newline is found, extract the frame preceding the newline and update the accumulator with the remaining trailing residue.

logic_steps.name: Protected JSON-RPC Decoding
logic_steps.description: Perform JSON decoding inside a protected call (pcall) for each parsed frame. If parsing fails, log the malformed frame loudly with rich context and continue processing instead of crashing.

logic_steps.name: UI Event Dispatch
logic_steps.description: Dispatch the successfully parsed JSON-RPC message object to UI handlers (e.g., nvim_client_ui_on_collapsed_block) using pure callback interfaces.

modification_tasks.task: TDD on_read: Write Busted unit tests in tests/test_nvim_client_network_on_read.lua to verify chunk accumulator handles split packets, multiple packets in one chunk, and invalid JSON gracefully (Guideline 12)
Stateful chunk accumulator: Design a robust per-connection string accumulator to handle partial network packet reads (Guideline 13)
Newline splitter: Parse and split the accumulator strictly on newline ('\n') characters, maintaining left-over fragments (Guideline 2)
Safe JSON decode & dispatch: Decode extracted frames inside protected blocks, dispatching valid objects to UI listeners while failing loudly on syntax errors (Guidelines 1, 5)

session_concurrency_flow.description: Client opens dedicated pipe socket connecting to UDS Daemon.

expected_side_effects.description: Monitors socket read streams and handles resource cleanup on client socket disconnect

dependencies.description: Daemon connection coroutine dispatches the incoming startup payload to the request router.
Dispatcher loads or resumes the stateful session record from disk.
Keystroke submission transmits prompt text and triggers the Mock Agent Executor.

expected_side_effects.description: Publishes events like telemetry:thought and telemetry:activity

dependencies.description: Cleans up session context and dictionary registration on socket EOF or client disconnect.

expected_side_effects.description: Cancels associated streaming tasks and closes writer

telemetry_streaming_flow.description: Mock executor publishes state/thought events to the event bus.

dependencies.description: Telemetry stream task intercepts event, calls codec to encode a JSON-RPC 2.0 streaming notification frame.
Client network loop reads socket frame, parses JSON-RPC notification, and triggers the UI on_collapsed_block render callback.

expected_side_effects.description: Mutates buffer to add collapsed virtual lines
